items.title: Censure motion against Tasmanian premier passes parliament

children.content: A censure motion against Jeremy Rockliff has passed, making him the first premier to be censured by the Tasmanian parliament.

gallery.altText: Jeremy Rockliff clasps his hands in parliament while leaning into the microphone.

thumbnail.altText: Jeremy Rockliff clasps his hands in parliament while leaning into the microphone.

avatar.alt: A woman with dark hair smiles.

items.title: AI factory 'brings into doubt' Marinus Link future

children.content: Tasmania's cool climate and access to renewable energy has made it an attractive place for AI data centre investment, but energy industry voices are warning that the business case for the Marinus Link project appears increasingly unviable if power-hungry players such as AI data centres continue to expand across the state.

image.altText: A large data centre sits prominantly in a forested landscape at Bell Bay.

items.title: Devils coaching choice both a blessing and a curse

children.content: Buckley? Longmire? Hinkley? The Tasmania Devils need to pick a coach to get them ready for their AFL entry, and the list of potential picks presents a dilemma.

image.altText: Nathan Buckley looks on at the 2018 premiership cup in the foreground with West Coast coloured ribbons

items.title: Sell-out shows, cult status line-up: How Dark Mofo catapults artists

children.content: Her headline show at Dark Mofo sold out in just over an hour, something Tasmanian Indigenous singer Miss Kaninna says couldn't have happened without the winter festival's ability to attract "massive, massive" music industry names.

gallery.altText: Miss Kaninna sits in an armchair in a room with red lighting.
Emi Doi stands on stage with two dancers and holds a microphone.
a music venue bathed in red light with a sign reading "dark"

thumbnail.altText: Miss Kaninna sits in an armchair in a room with red lighting.
Emi Doi stands on stage with two dancers and holds a microphone.
a music venue bathed in red light with a sign reading "dark"

items.title: Ex-teacher's aide jailed over Snapchat messages sent to teens

children.content: A former Tasmanian teacher's aide, who used social media to send sexually explicit content to teenagers, has been sentenced to 15 months' prison.

image.altText: A yellow Snapchat app logo on a black smart phone.

items.title: New fire truck arrives with multiple defects

children.content: It's a $600,000 fire-fighting truck, the first of 15 to enter service in Tasmania, but a list of faults has sent it back for warranty repairs.

image.altText: Two fire trucks positioned side by side.

items.title: Minister denies vomit gesture, says he was moving a breath-freshening strip

children.content: A Tasmanian Liberal politician denies he made a vomit gesture while listening to a female MP speak about integrity matters — saying he was moving a breath-freshening strip that had become stuck to the roof of his mouth.

image.altText: Kristie Johnston speaks in parliament as Gavin Pearce sits behind her and puts fingers in his mouth.

items.title: 'The answer … is yes': Premier confirms ex-ministers' legal fees are to fight the Integrity Commission

children.content: Jeremy Rockliff has confirmed in parliament that taxpayer-funded legal fees have been used by former ministers Jane Howlett and Madeleine Ogilvie to fight the Integrity Commission.

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff leans to his left while seated.

images.altText: Ivan Perišić leaps over Martin Baturina

articles.title: From refugee to Socceroos star: The grassroots club that gave Irankunda a go

children.content: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

image.altText: A young boy in a soccer uniform is running and kicking a ball.

articles.title: What to know about the Socceroos's next World Cup match

children.content: The Socceroos will face the USA in their second Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's what time you'll need to set your alarm.

image.altText: Connor Metcalfe and Nishan Velupillay hug on the pitch.

children.content: England kicks off its World Cup campaign with a nervy 4-2 victory against Croatia, the team that beat the Three Lions at the semifinal stage in 2018. Follow live.

updates.title: Kick off: Ghana vs Panama underway
Marcus Rashford seals it for England!

items.title: Wife and carer 'dumbfounded' by husband's aged care assessment

children.content: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he has been refused funding for more support.

image.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.

items.title: 'Cut the complexity': Watchdog moves to dramatically simplify power bills

children.content: Millions of Australians have been often unwittingly moved to complex and variable power tariffs. An energy watchdog says bills must be simplified.

image.altText: Shot of a smart meter in its box on the side of a house

items.title: HSBC 'gaslit' customers who lost life savings. Now it's fronting court over failures

children.content: HSBC customers who became victims of a long-running "spoofing" scam hope the bank will take full responsibility for its "system failures" in Federal Court this week.

image.altText: A woman with long black hair and a white shirt looks at camera.

items.title: KPMG the latest example of accountants being unaccountable

children.content: Bean counters have historically been viewed as dull, dull, dull. But since the turn of the century, accounting scandals have come thick and fast in Australia.

image.altText: People silhouetted against a white and blue KPMG logo.

items.title: There's blood in Blue water after NSW collapse in Origin II

children.content: New South Wales travelled to the MCG with the Origin series within their grasp but after the disaster that ensued success has never felt further away.

articles.title: Maroons star reveals dad's message before heartbreaking death

children.content: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui shares the advice his dad gave him before his death after a long battle with cancer.

image.altText: A man breaks through a tackle during a football game.

articles.title: Quick hits: Munster's HIA miracle and Klein listens to his bunker

children.content: Billy Slater resists the urge to play his trump card as the Maroons recover from an inexcusable early blunder. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin II.

image.altText: SOOII

articles.title: Maroons maul Blues in Melbourne to send Origin series to a decider

children.content: Queensland ignites with 36 points in the second half of State of Origin II to beat the Blues 44-24 and send the series to a decider.

items.title: Homeless woman feels safer living in vehicle than shared housing

children.content: Housing advocates say an increasing number of single, older women are choosing to live in vehicles while they wait for social housing.

image.altText: woman holding two dogs sitting on stairs of motorhome

items.title: Serial killer jailed for life over eight Long Island murders

children.content: A New York architect was sentenced to three consecutive life sentences plus 100 years after pleading guilty to the murders of at least eight women in Long Island's Gilgo Beach.

image.altText: Rex Heuermann, charged in the Gilgo Beach serial killings on Long Island

items.title: Weaponising homophobia, this Aussie horror is terrifying

children.content: Writer and director Adrian Chiarella's debut supernatural horror feature, Leviticus, is as romantic as it is scary.

gallery.altText: A young man holds another young man by the head

thumbnail.altText: A young man holds another young man by the head

items.title: Uni students tackle 'character-building' remote living through placements

children.content: University student Tarhnee says her survival kit for a nursing prac placement was food, a swag, a chair and a little table for her laptop.

image.altText: A small tent and camping chair.

items.title: In Peru, I meet a man risking it all to protect the Amazon

children.content: In Peru, I meet a man risking his life to end illegal gold mining in the Amazon, in the hope of saving his beloved town before it's too late.

image.altText: Manuel holda a while horse by its reins

items.title: Quiz: Grab your suitcase, we're going on a trip

children.content: Which airport hosts the world's largest indoor waterfall? What is the Swiss delicacy raclette?

image.altText: Brisbane cityscape and river in March 2021.

items.title: ASX to fall as US Federal Reserve leans towards rate hikes

children.content: The ASX is set to open down this morning, with the US Federal Reserve deciding to keep interest rates on hold, but a growing group of committee members predict a rate hike this year.

image.altText: An electronic billboard showing stock prices is seen in a window reflection.

items.title: Katherine couple still sleeping on verandah three months after major flooding

children.content: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

gallery.altText: A woman sits on a bed outdoors, patting a dog.
A view from above of brown water and a road running through it

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a bed outdoors, patting a dog.

items.title: Sydney-to-London unveiled as first route for Qantas's Project Sunrise

children.content: The flights, set to begin take off from next year, will mark the first time flights from the east coast will complete the Kangaroo Route without a stop over.

image.altText: A large Qantas plane sitting by a jetbridge at an airport.

items.title: Convicted paedophile cleared to run family‑friendly Sydney apartment blocks

children.content: The NSW government has authorised a convicted paedophile to manage nearly a dozen apartment buildings across Sydney, including those popular with young families.

image.altText: A composite image of Jeffery, an older man, overlayed on an apartment block.

items.title: David Littleproud takes on 'parasitic' insurers' in the bush

children.content: The MP has referred insurers, which he calls "corporate cancer", to the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission.

image.altText: maranoa mp david littleproud standing outside in front of a fence

items.title: Don't expect a post-budget $1,250 boost this tax time

children.content: There was a lot of talk about the $1,000 instant tax reduction and the $250 Working Australians Tax Offset in the federal budget. But they won't be in play at tax time this year.

image.altText: Woman with head in hands with tax return on screen in the background.

items.title: Underground discovery could help save one of the world's rarest mammals

children.content: One of the world's most critically endangered animals may be able to live in more areas than conservationists realised.

gallery.altText: A wombat walks out of a burrow

thumbnail.altText: A wombat walks out of a burrow

items.title: Wanted fugitive fled Australia six days after home raided by NSW Police

children.content: The alleged gunman slipped the net and fled to Lebanon days after he was paid hundreds of thousands of dollars to take out a Sydney underworld figure in 2022.

image.altText: Mugshot of Rabeih Baltagi

items.title: Couple sentenced for neglecting more than 100 pets

children.content: A South Australian couple is barred from owning pets after mistreating more than 100 animals, including cats, a dog, chickens and other birds.

image.altText: A brown dog in a cage stands in her cage next to a water bowl filled with green algae and faeces laying around her.

items.title: Father of 17yo killed in e-bike crash speaks after teen driver sentenced

children.content: A Gold Coast father whose son died in an electric bike crash has spoken out after the driver was sentenced, saying the devices need better regulation.

items.title: Injured toddler's ordeal highlights risks of weather radar blackspot

children.content: When two-year-old Teddy suffered a suspected broken leg on his family's outback property, the journey to hospital was complicated by a lack of reliable weather forecasting.

image.altText: A young boy with a cast on his leg sits on a lounge chair at home.

items.title: Perry makes ominous return with the ball in T20 World Cup trouncing

children.content: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia waltzes home to victory over Bangladesh in the women's T20 World Cup.

image.altText: Ellyse Perry celebrates dressed in her cricket colours

items.title: Mangione to argue he suffered 'extreme emotional disturbance' in murder trial

children.content: The 28-year-old is accused of killing an American health insurance executive. If successful, his defence could see him convicted of manslaughter rather than murder.

image.altText: Luigi Mangione in a suit and shirt sitting at the defence table holding an envelope.

items.title: Bill restricting late-term abortions fails to pass SA lower house

children.content: South Australia's lower house has voted against a bill to restrict access to abortions past 25 weeks. The legislation was defeated 36 votes to 9.

image.altText: A woman in a black west with a green and blue ribbon pinned to her chest walks up stone steps.

items.title: Cambodia says 310,000 suspected scammers left country amid crackdown

children.content: A report by Amnesty International casts doubt on the impact of Cambodia's high-profile scam industry crackdown, finding authorities failed to dismantle the "vast majority" of operations.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of a prison-like compound with a series of long buildings with blue roofs surrounding a square centre building.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of a prison-like compound with a series of long buildings with blue roofs surrounding a square centre building.
